prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(initialize_traveler_case primary_traveler_lead)
(initialize_traveler_case companion_traveler_1)
(initialize_traveler_case application_case_1)
(initialize_traveler_case application_case_2)
(assign_permit_option primary_traveler_lead permit_option_1)
(assign_permit_option companion_traveler_1 permit_option_2)
(assign_permit_option application_case_2 permit_option_3)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(verify_primary_condition_profile_by_rule primary_traveler_lead condition_profile_primary_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(associate_service_channel_with_case primary_traveler_lead service_channel_consulate)
(record_primary_condition_and_prepare_product primary_traveler_lead condition_profile_primary_1 service_channel_consulate)
(revert_jurisdiction_link primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(release_service_channel_from_case primary_traveler_lead service_channel_consulate)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case companion_traveler_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation companion_traveler_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(verify_companion_condition_profile_by_rule companion_traveler_1 condition_profile_companion_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(associate_service_channel_with_case companion_traveler_1 service_channel_consulate)
(record_companion_condition_and_prepare_product companion_traveler_1 condition_profile_companion_1 service_channel_consulate)
(revert_jurisdiction_link companion_traveler_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(release_service_channel_from_case companion_traveler_1 service_channel_consulate)
(assemble_permit_product_variant primary_traveler_lead companion_traveler_1 condition_profile_primary_1 condition_profile_companion_1 permit_product_variant_1)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(mark_product_variant_ready_for_document_binding permit_product_variant_1 primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(revert_jurisdiction_link primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_primary_traveler_eligibility primary_traveler_lead permit_product_variant_1)
(finalize_companion_traveler_eligibility companion_traveler_1 permit_product_variant_1)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case application_case_2 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation application_case_2 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(attach_requirement_item_to_product application_case_2 requirement_item_invitation permit_product_variant_1)
(finalize_case_prechecks application_case_2 requirement_item_invitation permit_product_variant_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(revert_jurisdiction_link application_case_2 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(attach_health_certificate_to_case application_case_2 health_certificate_vaccine)
(associate_service_channel_with_case application_case_2 service_channel_consulate)
(initiate_specialized_checks_with_health_certificate application_case_2 health_certificate_vaccine service_channel_consulate requirement_item_invitation permit_product_variant_1)
(release_service_channel_from_case application_case_2 service_channel_consulate)
(attach_clearance_certificate_to_case application_case_2 clearance_certificate_1)
(apply_clearance_certificate_to_case application_case_2 clearance_certificate_1 requirement_item_invitation permit_product_variant_1)
(finalize_case_decision_no_fee application_case_2)
(remove_clearance_certificate_from_case application_case_2 clearance_certificate_1)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case application_case_2 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(consume_verification_token application_case_2 verification_token_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_case_determination_and_allocate_option application_case_2 permit_option_3 verification_token_1)
(revert_jurisdiction_link application_case_2 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(assign_permit_option application_case_1 permit_option_3)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case application_case_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_rule_interpretation application_case_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(confirm_sponsorship_for_case application_case_1 sponsorship_document_invitation destination_jurisdiction_1)
(associate_service_channel_with_case application_case_1 service_channel_consulate)
(process_sponsorship_via_channel application_case_1 service_channel_consulate)
(attach_clearance_certificate_to_case application_case_1 clearance_certificate_1)
(confirm_clearance_for_case application_case_1 clearance_certificate_1)
(finalize_case_decision_after_sponsorship application_case_1)
(revert_jurisdiction_link application_case_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(consume_verification_token primary_traveler_lead verification_token_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_primary_traveler_determination_and_allocate_option primary_traveler_lead permit_option_1 verification_token_1)
(revert_jurisdiction_link primary_traveler_lead destination_jurisdiction_1)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case companion_traveler_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(consume_verification_token companion_traveler_1 verification_token_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_companion_traveler_determination_and_allocate_option companion_traveler_1 permit_option_2 verification_token_1)
(revert_jurisdiction_link companion_traveler_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case application_case_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(consume_verification_token application_case_1 verification_token_1 destination_jurisdiction_1)
(finalize_case_determination_and_allocate_option application_case_1 permit_option_3 verification_token_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a planning domain for destination permit adjudication. The goal is to achieve (permit_determination_flag) for primary_traveler_lead, companion_traveler_1, application_case_1, and application_case_2.


## Examining Planning Domain  
- The task centers on a planning domain for destination permit adjudication.  
- The goal is to determine permits for primary_traveler_lead, companion_traveler_1, and two application cases: application_case_1 and application_case_2.

## Examining Planning Domain  
- Eligibility_finalized is required for both travelers (primary_traveler_lead and companion_traveler_1) and cases (application_case_1, application_case_2).  
- Different paths exist to achieve eligibility_finalized, varying by traveler or case, involving conditions like product assembly and case decisions.  

## Identifying Shared Resources  
- Only one destination_jurisdiction_1 and one service_channel_consulate are available, shared across multiple entities.  
- Actions like bind_jurisdiction_rule_to_case consume resources, requiring reversion to reuse for other entities.  

## Exploring Eligibility Paths  
- For travelers, eligibility_finalized involves finalize_primary_traveler_eligibility, needing conditions like product_variant_reserved and document_binding readiness.  
- For cases, eligibility_finalized can stem from finalize_case_decision variants, such as no-fee decisions or post-aggregation.  

## Checking Initialization Steps  
- Initialize_case is likely needed for each entity: primary_traveler_lead, companion_traveler_1, application_case_1, and application_case_2.  
- Assign_permit_option follows, with three options available for allocation.

## Examining Permit Option Constraints  
- The system has only three permit options available, but four entities (primary_traveler_lead, companion_traveler_1, application_case_1, application_case_2) need option_selected.  
- Assigning permit options consumes them, and there's no revert option, creating a resource bottleneck early in the process.  

## Exploring Workaround Strategies  
- Finalize_determination_and_allocate_option could release a permit option, allowing reuse, but this happens at the end, complicating the flow.  
- One idea is to process one entity fully to determination, releasing its option for the next, but travelers' product assembly requires both to be ready, needing options simultaneously.  

## Analyzing Path Dependencies  
- All paths for travelers and cases require option_selected early, including for jurisdiction binding and rule interpretation.  
- Even alternative paths like sponsorship for cases still need option_selected, leaving no clear way to bypass the option limit.

## Analyzing Specialized Checks  
- Specialized checks with health certificates require prechecks, health certificate attachment, and rule interpretation linked to a product variant without companion evidence.  
- Variants exist for cases with or without product variant flags, affecting how clearance and fees are applied based on evidence conditions.

## Examining Clearance and Fee Processes  
- Applying clearance certificates to cases requires specialized checks completed, with variants depending on primary or companion evidence flags, affecting fee attachment.  
- Finalizing case decisions involves no-fee options or fee-based paths, requiring conditions like fee availability and traveler conditions to be satisfied.

## Exploring Sponsorship Path Alternatives  
- The sponsorship path bypasses product variant requirements, using sponsorship documents and channel association for clearance, suitable for one case.  
- For application_case_1, sponsorship is viable with an available invitation, while application_case_2 lacks the necessary affidavit, forcing a product variant path.

## Sequencing Entity Processing  
- Process primary traveler (PT) first: assign permit option, bind jurisdiction, finalize interpretation, verify, and associate service channel.  
- Record condition for PT to set ready and satisfied, then revert jurisdiction and release channel for reuse, ensuring efficiency.  

## Reusing Resources for Companion  
- Repeat the process for companion traveler (CT) using the same jurisdiction and channel after PT is ready, maintaining resource availability.  
- Both PT and CT need to be ready and satisfied before assembling the permit product variant, linking profiles and reserving the variant.  

## Binding Jurisdiction Post-Assembly  
- After assembly, re-bind jurisdiction to PT for document binding, as it's required for marking the variant ready, despite earlier reversion.  
- Finalize eligibility for both PT and CT once the variant is ready for document binding, without needing jurisdiction linked for the final step.

## Processing Application Case 2 (AC2)  
- AC2 has rule interpretation (RI_i) linked to product variant (PV) without companion evidence flags, using standard assembly.  
- Attaching clearance certificate to AC2 requires interpretation and availability, marking it specialized after application.

## Finalizing AC2 Without Fees  
- AC2 meets conditions for no-fee finalization with all special requirements satisfied, finalizing eligibility without policy exceptions.  
- The process completes decision recording for AC2, ensuring no fee options or flags are attached.

## Managing Permit Options for AC1  
- AC1 lacks an assigned option, requiring release of an option like PO3 from AC2 after its determination.  
- Re-binding jurisdiction to entities like PT or AC2 is needed for verification token consumption, ensuring eligibility and option allocation.

## Exploring Sponsorship for AC1  
- Sponsorship for AC1 requires interpreting rules, binding jurisdiction, and confirming with sponsorship documents, marking them unavailable.  
- Processing sponsorship needs channel association, setting the stage for clearance confirmation without fee attachments.

## Preparing Companion Traveler (CT)

- Release the service channel from the primary traveler lead to free it for CT processing.
- Bind jurisdiction rules to CT and finalize rule interpretation for consistency.

## Verifying CT Eligibility

- Verify CT conditions using the companion profile and associated service channel.
- Record conditions and prepare the product, then revert jurisdiction to optimize resource use.

## Assembling Permit Product

- Assemble the permit product variant for both primary traveler and CT once conditions are satisfied.
- Mark the variant ready for document binding after re-binding jurisdiction to the primary traveler.

## Finalizing Traveler Eligibility

- Revert jurisdiction after marking the variant ready to streamline processes.
- Finalize eligibility for both primary traveler and CT, completing traveler preparations.

## Processing Application Case 2 (AC2)

- Bind jurisdiction to AC2 and finalize rule interpretation for accurate processing.
- Attach requirement items to the product variant, ensuring all prerequisites are met.

## Completing AC2 Prechecks

- Finalize prechecks for AC2 with jurisdiction still linked, confirming readiness.
- Optionally revert jurisdiction to prepare for further steps, maintaining flexibility.

## Attaching Certificates to AC2

- Attach health and clearance certificates to AC2, initiating specialized checks.
- Apply clearance certificate to the case, ensuring all special conditions are linked.

## Finalizing AC2 Decision

- Complete AC2 decision-making process without fees, meeting all eligibility criteria.
- Release the clearance certificate from AC2, making it available for other uses like AC1.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
